pattern: Repeatedly dividing by the base peels off one digit at a time, and summing those remainders gives the base-k digit sum without ever forming the full representation.
hints: You do not need the actual base-k string; you only need the sum of its digits.
Each remainder of n modulo k is one digit; add it up while you keep dividing.
Loop: total += n % k; n //= k; until n is zero.

edge_inputs.description: n equal to base gives digit sum one

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 100)
    k = rng.randint(2, 10)
    return [n, k]
